explanationZhTw: 「dream of」在這裡表示「夢見、在睡夢中想到」，不是指「做夢想要」的抽象用法。
generatedExample: Last night I was dreaming of my childhood home.

explanationZhTw: 「disappeared」在這裡不是單純「不見了」，而是指母親失蹤、從他的生活中消失。
generatedExample: My keys disappeared after the meeting, so I checked every chair and pocket.

explanationZhTw: 「statuesque」形容人高挑、姿態優雅而且有雕像般的氣質。
generatedExample: The model had a statuesque presence that made her stand out in every photo.

explanationZhTw: 「magnificent」表示非常漂亮、出色或令人印象深刻。
generatedExample: They moved into a house with a magnificent view of the sea.

explanationZhTw: 「vaguely」表示記得不清楚、模糊地。
generatedExample: I vaguely remember meeting her at a conference years ago.

explanationZhTw: 「spectacles」是較正式的「眼鏡」說法。
generatedExample: He took off his spectacles and cleaned them carefully.

explanationZhTw: 「swallowed up」在這裡不是字面上的「被吞下去」，而是指被某個事件、體制或大規模行動完全吞沒、消失。
generatedExample: The small village was swallowed up by the expanding city.

explanationZhTw: 「purges」在政治語境中指清洗行動，通常是把被懷疑的人從組織或社會中排除、逮捕或消滅。
generatedExample: The regime launched purges to remove anyone it considered disloyal.

explanationZhTw: 「feeble」在這裡表示嬰兒很虛弱、體弱，不是單純「小」而已，帶有無力、病弱的感覺。
generatedExample: The feeble baby needed constant care from the nurses.

explanationZhTw: 「watchful」指警覺、留意周遭的樣子，這裡用來描寫嬰兒的眼神帶有持續觀察、敏銳注視的意味。
generatedExample: The guard kept watchful eyes on the entrance.

explanationZhTw: 「subterranean」是「地下的、地底下的」。這裡用得很具象，強調那個地方深埋在地底之下。
generatedExample: We explored a subterranean tunnel beneath the castle.

explanationZhTw: 「saloon」在這裡不是一般常見的「酒吧」，而是指船上的公共艙室或休息區，屬於偏特殊的語境用法。
generatedExample: We waited in the saloon while the ship crossed the bay.

explanationZhTw: 「sucked down」是被往下吸、往下拉的意思，帶有強烈的被動力量感。
generatedExample: The swimmer felt sucked down by the heavy current.

explanationZhTw: 「reproach」在這裡是「責備、怨懟」的意思。這裡說他們臉上和心裡都沒有責怪，凸顯出一種無聲的接受。
generatedExample: She spoke without reproach, even though she was disappointed.

explanationZhTw: 「the unavoidable order of things」表示「無法避免的事物規律／命運安排」。這裡不是字面上的「排序」，而是指殘酷但必然的世界法則。
generatedExample: He accepted the loss as part of the unavoidable order of things.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「打到」的意思，而是「突然讓溫斯頓想到／意識到」。
generatedExample: The reason for the delay suddenly struck me after I checked the schedule.

explanationZhTw: 指彼此支持、在困難時互相扶持。
generatedExample: When the news was bad, the whole team stood by one another.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻用法，表示強烈刺痛他的內心，讓他非常心痛。
generatedExample: The old letter tore at his heart every time he read it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指她為了忠誠這個信念，放棄了自己的利益甚至人生。
generatedExample: She sacrificed herself to protect her family.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指情感中的莊重感、尊嚴與深度，帶有特殊語感。
generatedExample: The speech had a dignity of emotion that silenced the room.

explanationZhTw: 短而有彈性的草皮；這裡指腳下的草地柔軟、鬆彈。
generatedExample: The children ran across the short springy turf without sinking in.

explanationZhTw: 斜斜照射的陽光。
generatedExample: The slanting rays of the sun made the hills look golden.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「鍍金」，而是文學用法，表示陽光把地面照得像金色一樣。
generatedExample: The setting sun gilded the tops of the trees.

explanationZhTw: 反覆出現、再度浮現。這裡指同一景象一再在夢中出現。
generatedExample: That strange song recurred in her mind all day.

explanationZhTw: 清醒時的想法；和做夢時相對。
generatedExample: In his waking thoughts, he could not explain why the place felt so familiar.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「被兔子咬過的」，這裡形容草地被兔子啃食得零零落落。
generatedExample: The rabbit-bitten lawn looked patchy and uneven.

explanationZhTw: 牧場、放牧的草地。
generatedExample: The sheep were grazing in the pasture behind the barn.

explanationZhTw: 人或動物踩出來的小路、足跡路徑。
generatedExample: A narrow foot-track led through the field to the river.

explanationZhTw: 參差不齊的、破破爛爛的；這裡形容樹籬外形不整齊。
generatedExample: The ragged edges of the paper showed it had been torn quickly.

explanationZhTw: 樹枝，通常指較大的枝條。
generatedExample: Birds were sitting on the boughs above the path.

explanationZhTw: 輕輕搖擺、擺動。
generatedExample: The tall grass was swaying in the wind.

explanationZhTw: 微弱地、輕微地。
generatedExample: She could faintly hear music from the next room.

explanationZhTw: 密集成團；這裡形容葉子濃密地聚在一起。
generatedExample: The smoke rose in dense masses above the factory.

explanationZhTw: 看不見、在視線之外。
generatedExample: The village was just out of sight behind the hill.

explanationZhTw: 流動緩慢的小溪／小河。
generatedExample: They crossed a slow-moving stream on a small wooden bridge.

explanationZhTw: 一種淡水小魚，中文常譯作「雅羅魚」。
generatedExample: Small dace darted around the rocks in the shallow water.

explanationZhTw: `tore off` 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「迅速脫掉」或「猛地扯下」衣服，不是一般字面上的「撕下」。
generatedExample: She tore off her jacket when she got inside.

explanationZhTw: `disdainfully` 表示「輕蔑地、鄙夷地」，帶有不屑一顧的態度。
generatedExample: He looked at the old rules disdainfully and ignored them.

explanationZhTw: `aroused` 在這裡是「激起、引起」的意思，指引發某種感覺或慾望。
generatedExample: The speech aroused great interest in the audience.

explanationZhTw: `overwhelmed` 在這裡表示「強烈地壓倒、使完全充滿」，不是單純的「打敗」。
generatedExample: I was overwhelmed by gratitude when they helped me.

explanationZhTw: `gesture` 這裡不是一般的「手勢」而已，而是帶有象徵意義的動作，表示一種態度或宣示。
generatedExample: His refusal was seen as a political gesture.

explanationZhTw: `annihilate` 意思是「徹底摧毀、使完全消失」，比一般的 destroy 更強烈。
generatedExample: A single mistake can annihilate months of progress.

explanationZhTw: `swept into nothingness` 這裡是形象化說法，意思是「被一下子掃除得無影無蹤」或「完全抹去」。
generatedExample: The old fears were swept into nothingness after the truth came out.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的虛構裝置，兼具電視與監視功能。這裡不是一般的電視，而是帶有監控意涵的「電幕」。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the apartment never seemed to stop watching them.

explanationZhTw: 形容聲音大到刺耳、震耳欲聾。
generatedExample: An ear-splitting alarm woke everyone in the building.

explanationZhTw: 指「起床時間」；這裡是帶有制度感的固定作息說法，不只是一般的起床。
generatedExample: For the campers, sunrise was getting-up time.

explanationZhTw: 用力猛拉、硬拽。這裡強調他幾乎是把身體「扯」離床鋪。
generatedExample: She wrenched the door open and ran outside.

explanationZhTw: 形容因疼痛或咳嗽而彎成一團、蜷縮起來。
generatedExample: He doubled up with stomach pain after lunch.

explanationZhTw: 指急促、用力吸氣的動作；這裡表示他必須一口一口大口喘氣。
generatedExample: After the sprint, she was taking gasps of air.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `yapped` 不是單純指狗吠，而是形容人用又急又尖、帶點粗魯的聲音說話。
generatedExample: The clerk yapped instructions at us before we could even sit down.

explanationZhTw: `piercing` 在這裡表示「尖銳刺耳的」，用來形容聲音很高、很容易刺入耳朵。
generatedExample: We heard a piercing whistle from the hallway.

explanationZhTw: `Take your places` 是固定指令，意思是「到指定位置站好、就位」。
generatedExample: The director told the actors to take their places before the curtain went up.

explanationZhTw: 迅速站直並立正；常用來形容聽到命令後立刻擺出正式站姿。
generatedExample: When the officer entered, the recruits sprang to attention.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》裡的專有裝置名，指會播放影像、也可能監視人的螢幕；不是一般日常電視。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the corner never seemed to stop watching.

explanationZhTw: 表示「有點年輕、偏年輕」；語氣比 young 更不絕對。
generatedExample: A youngish man asked for directions outside the station.

explanationZhTw: 瘦得皮包骨的，常帶有不健康或營養不良的感覺。
generatedExample: The scrawny cat hid under the car.

explanationZhTw: 肌肉發達的；描述身體結實、有明顯肌肉。
generatedExample: He has a muscular build from years of swimming.

explanationZhTw: 束腰外衣、寬鬆上衣；常見於歷史或制服語境。
generatedExample: The actor wore a simple tunic for the stage play.

explanationZhTw: 運動鞋；較老式或較具時代感的說法。
generatedExample: She packed her gym-shoes before leaving for camp.

explanationZhTw: 迅速而嚴厲地說出；這裡不是指敲擊，而是表示她用很硬、很急的口氣喊出指令。
generatedExample: She rapped out the order and marched ahead.

explanationZhTw: 慢慢來，不要急；用來叫對方放慢速度、不要趕時間。
generatedExample: Take your time and check your answers carefully.

explanationZhTw: 同志、同伴；在政治語境裡常用來稱呼同一陣營或同一組織的人。
generatedExample: The speaker greeted the comrades at the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 讓它更有精神、更有活力；這裡是在催促大家動作再有勁一點。
generatedExample: The coach told us to put a bit of life into the warm-up.

explanationZhTw: 片語「drive out of」在這裡是「把……趕出、驅散」的意思；表示咳嗽的疼痛幾乎把夢的印象從他的腦中趕走。
generatedExample: The loud alarm drove sleep out of my mind.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「機械地」，而是指「不假思索、像自動反應一樣」。
generatedExample: He nodded mechanically while thinking about something else.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「往後思考」，而是指努力回想、倒著追溯到更早的記憶。
generatedExample: She tried to think her way backward to the first day she met him.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 dim 不是「昏暗」，而是指記憶或年代「模糊、不清楚」。
generatedExample: She could only recall a dim period of her childhood.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是指記憶、印象「逐漸變模糊、消失」，不是單純的顏色褪去。
generatedExample: After a few years, the details of the trip faded.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 sharpness 指「清晰度、銳利感」；意思是回憶變得不再清楚。
generatedExample: Without photos, the memory gradually lost its sharpness.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 atmosphere 不是「空氣」，而是指某段經歷的「氛圍、感受、現場味道」。
generatedExample: The old photograph recaptured the atmosphere of the market.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指記憶中的「空白時段」，也就是完全想不起來的時間段。
generatedExample: There were blank periods in his memory after the accident.

explanationZhTw: 表示「確實、無疑地」，用來強調記憶不清但確定性很高。
generatedExample: I definitely heard someone knocking at the door.

explanationZhTw: 表示「明顯的、顯而易見的」。
generatedExample: It was evident that he had studied hard for the test.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「一段時間／間隔」，不是音樂裡的音程。
generatedExample: We had a short interval between classes.

explanationZhTw: 指「空襲」；在戰爭語境中是對城市的空中轟炸。
generatedExample: The family ran to the shelter during the air raid.

explanationZhTw: 成語，表示「讓所有人措手不及／出其不意」。
generatedExample: The sudden announcement took everyone by surprise.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `fall on` 表示「落到／擊中」，指炸彈落在某地。
generatedExample: A tree branch had fallen on the car during the storm.

explanationZhTw: 表示「緊抓著、攥著」，帶出緊張或用力的動作。
generatedExample: She was clutching her bag as she walked through the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「使……疲憊」，比一般 `tired` 更書面、較少見。
generatedExample: The long climb wearied him before he reached the top.

explanationZhTw: 指「嗚咽、抽搭地哭」，通常是小孩或受驚的人發出的聲音。
generatedExample: The puppy kept whimpering until it found its owner.

explanationZhTw: 表示「走出、出現」，這裡是從地下空間出來。
generatedExample: After the meeting, she emerged with a clear plan.

explanationZhTw: 在英國，`Tube` 指倫敦地鐵，所以是「地鐵站」。
generatedExample: We met outside the Tube station before dinner.

explanationZhTw: 「stone-flagged floor」指鋪著石板的地板；這裡是描述場景的詞組，`flagged` 不是「旗幟」的意思，而是「鋪石板的」。
generatedExample: The market had a stone-flagged floor that stayed cool in summer.

explanationZhTw: `found themselves` 在這裡不是單純「找到自己」，而是「不知不覺處於某種情況／最後只能待在某處」。
generatedExample: We found ourselves waiting in the rain for an hour.

explanationZhTw: `side by side` 表示「並排、肩並肩地」。
generatedExample: The two friends sat side by side during the concert.

explanationZhTw: `pushed back` 是片語動詞，這裡指把帽子往後推，使頭髮露出來。
generatedExample: She pushed back her hair before speaking.

explanationZhTw: `reeked of` 表示「散發出強烈難聞的氣味」，也可引申為「充滿某種明顯特徵」。這裡是非常重的酒味。
generatedExample: His jacket reeked of smoke after the fire.

explanationZhTw: `welling from` 在這裡不是一般日常用法，意思是「湧出、冒出」。它強調液體慢慢往上湧。
generatedExample: Tears were welling from her eyes as she listened.

explanationZhTw: `suffering under` 在此表示「受……折磨／背負著……痛苦」，比單純的 `suffer` 更強調壓力或情緒的壓迫感。
generatedExample: He was suffering under the weight of his debts.

explanationZhTw: `grasped` 這裡不是「抓住」，而是「理解、領會」。屬於常見詞的情境義轉換。
generatedExample: I finally grasped why the plan had failed.

explanationZhTw: `beyond forgiveness` 表示「無法被原諒的」，語氣很重，常用來描述極嚴重的錯事。
generatedExample: What he did was beyond forgiveness.

explanationZhTw: `remedied` 是 `remedy` 的動詞，意思是「補救、修正、解決」。這裡表示那件事已經無法挽回。
generatedExample: The error was quickly remedied by the engineer.

explanationZhTw: 這是口語裡的非標準說法，意思接近「本來不應該／不該」。比起標準的 `should not have`，帶有更口語、老派的味道。
generatedExample: You didn't ought to tell him about the surprise party.

explanationZhTw: `come of` 這裡表示「由……造成、源自……的結果」。整句 `That's what comes of ...` 常用來說「這就是……帶來的後果」。
generatedExample: That's what comes of leaving your bike unlocked.

explanationZhTw: `buggers` 是英式粗俗語，指「混蛋、討厭鬼、傢伙」，這裡是強烈抱怨或咒罵的語氣。
generatedExample: Those buggers broke the window and ran off.

explanationZhTw: 「buggers」在這裡是帶有粗俗、鄙視語氣的口語用法，指人時有「傢伙、混蛋、那些人」的感覺。放在這句中，它不是字面意思，而是說話者對某些人帶有情緒性的稱呼。
generatedExample: Those buggers kept changing the meeting time without telling anyone.

explanationZhTw: 「didn't ought to」是非標準、帶口語感的表達，意思接近「不應該」。在這裡它表示「哪些人不該信任」，但語法上不是一般現代標準英語最自然的說法。
generatedExample: He didn't ought to have said that in front of everyone.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「literally」是在加強語氣，表示戰爭幾乎沒有真正中斷過，不是字面上每一秒都同一場戰爭，而是整體上一直持續。
generatedExample: The noise from the factory was literally continuous all night.

explanationZhTw: 「strictly speaking」表示「嚴格來說、準確來說」，用來補充前面的說法，指出更精確的細節。
generatedExample: Strictly speaking, the meeting starts at 9:00, not 9:15.

explanationZhTw: 「trace out」在這裡不是單純「畫出」，而是指「梳理、追查並重建」整段歷史脈絡。
generatedExample: The researcher tried to trace out how the rumor started.

explanationZhTw: 「made mention of」是正式說法，意思是「提到、談及」。這裡強調當時沒有任何記錄曾提過其他陣營。
generatedExample: The report made mention of several possible risks.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「alignment」不是一般的「對齊」，而是指政治或軍事上的「結盟、站在同一邊」。
generatedExample: The two countries changed their alignment after the election.

explanationZhTw: 「in alliance with」表示「與……結盟」，常用於國家、組織或團體之間的合作關係。
generatedExample: The small nation was in alliance with its neighbors for defense.

explanationZhTw: 「utterance」指「說出口的話、表述」，比一般的 speech 更正式，也帶有「發言內容」的意思。
generatedExample: Every utterance from the witness was carefully recorded.

explanationZhTw: 「furtive」有「偷偷摸摸、隱密的」意思；「furtive knowledge」就是偷偷知道、不能公開說的資訊。
generatedExample: She kept a furtive knowledge of the plan from everyone else.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「換搭檔」，而是把國家之間的結盟關係比喻成「換伙伴」，意思是外交立場改變。
generatedExample: After the scandal, the company made a sudden change of partners.

explanationZhTw: 「enemy of the moment」指「當下的敵人、暫時被指定為敵人的對象」，帶有政治宣傳的含義，不只是一般的敵人。
generatedExample: In the debate, the enemy of the moment became the blame for every problem.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「一萬次」，而是強調他已經反覆想過很多次，帶有誇張語氣。
generatedExample: For the ten thousandth time, I told him not to touch the settings.

explanationZhTw: 指身體大幅扭動、旋轉，常用來形容跳舞或做動作時的擺動。
generatedExample: The dancers were gyrating to the loud music.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，表示強行插手、干預過去，並非真的把手伸進過去。
generatedExample: The new law seemed to thrust the government hand into every part of daily life.

explanationZhTw: 表示「只是、僅僅」，用來把後面的事物說得比較不嚴重或不重要。
generatedExample: It was mere coincidence that we met at the station.

explanationZhTw: 表示與某方結盟、合作或共同站在同一陣線，常用於國家、組織或政治力量之間。
generatedExample: The two countries were in alliance with each other during the negotiations.

explanationZhTw: 意識；指人對自己、周圍環境和內在狀態的知覺與認知。
generatedExample: She lost consciousness for a few seconds after the fall.

explanationZhTw: 徹底摧毀、完全消滅；比一般的 destroy 語氣更強。
generatedExample: The fire could have annihilated the entire building.

explanationZhTw: 表示某件事被當成既成事實並流傳到歷史裡，帶有「被重複說到最後變成真的」的諷刺感。
generatedExample: The rumor passed into history as if it had always been true.

explanationZhTw: 可改變的；能夠被修改或變動的。
generatedExample: The schedule is alterable if the client requests changes.

explanationZhTw: 從永遠到永遠；強調時間無止境，常見於文學或宗教語境。
generatedExample: Their friendship seemed to last from everlasting to everlasting.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的造詞，指同時接受兩個互相矛盾的觀念，並把它們都當成真。
generatedExample: It felt like doublethink to praise transparency while hiding the facts.

explanationZhTw: 這是軍隊口令，意思是「稍息／放鬆站姿」，不是字面上的「站得容易」。
generatedExample: The sergeant told the recruits to stand easy after the drill.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指狗吠，而是指「厲聲地說、粗聲喝斥」。
generatedExample: He barked a quick order and walked out of the room.

explanationZhTw: 表示「和氣地、親切地」，是比較少見的副詞。
generatedExample: She smiled genially and invited us to sit down.

explanationZhTw: 形容非常複雜、像迷宮一樣難以理解。
generatedExample: The report was buried in a labyrinthine system of approvals.

explanationZhTw: 奧威爾筆下的概念，指同時接受兩個互相矛盾的想法，卻都相信。
generatedExample: The novel explores the logic of doublethink in a propaganda state.

explanationZhTw: 同時發生或存在。
generatedExample: She could listen and take notes simultaneously.

explanationZhTw: 彼此矛盾、不能同時成立。
generatedExample: Their explanations were contradictory and hard to trust.

explanationZhTw: 公開否認、拒絕承認，帶有強烈否定意味。
generatedExample: He repudiated the allegations in a public statement.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「法定監護人」，而是指「守護者／捍衛者」，帶有政治修辭意味。
generatedExample: The constitution is meant to be a guardian of individual rights.

explanationZhTw: 細微而難察覺的巧妙差異或複雜處。
generatedExample: The humor depends on a subtlety that many readers miss.

explanationZhTw: 催眠狀態；此處比喻思想被操控、失去自我察覺的狀態。
generatedExample: The stage show used hypnosis as part of the performance.

explanationZhTw: 指女教師或女教練，現在較少見，帶有較正式或文學色彩。
generatedExample: The instructress told the class to open their books.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「打電話提醒」，而是指讓他們立正、集中注意，準備聽指令。
generatedExample: The coach called the players to attention before practice started.

explanationZhTw: 表示「熱情地、興奮地」，用來描述說話或做事時很有熱忱。
generatedExample: She nodded enthusiastically when she heard the good news.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「同志、同伴」的稱呼，常見於政治或集體訓練語境，不是一般日常的「朋友」。
generatedExample: The officer addressed the recruits as comrades.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 bring on 表示「引發、導致」；指這個動作把另一陣咳嗽引出來。
generatedExample: Skipping breakfast kept bringing on headaches every morning.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 meditations 不是宗教冥想，而是指「沉思、思緒」。
generatedExample: Her meditations on the problem were interrupted by a phone call.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡 establish 是「證明、確認」事實，不只是一般的「建立」。
generatedExample: Without records, it was hard to establish what really happened.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 figured as 是「被描寫成、被當作」的意思。
generatedExample: In the article, she figured as the main critic of the policy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻性的說法，指把歷史往更早年代硬推，暗示時間線被竄改。
generatedExample: The legend was pushed backwards in time to make the hero seem older.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 fabulous 不是「極好的」，而是「傳奇般的、虛構得誇張的」。
generatedExample: The story had grown so fabulous that nobody believed every detail.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 current 是「流通中的、正在使用的」；不是只指『目前的』。
generatedExample: That expression was current in the 1980s.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，表示「準確指出、說中」問題或事實的核心。
generatedExample: I knew the report was wrong, but I couldn't put my finger on why.

explanationZhTw: 指有文件或資料可驗證的證據。
generatedExample: The lawyer asked for documentary proof of the agreement.

explanationZhTw: 指把事實改假、竄改或捏造。
generatedExample: The audit exposed the falsification of several records.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，形容人尖酸、愛刁難、脾氣不好，這裡是在描述那個聲音聽起來很刻薄。
generatedExample: Her shrewish tone made the room feel tense.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的虛構裝置，兼具電視與監視功能；這是小說世界裡的特定用法。
generatedExample: The telescreen watched every move in the apartment.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是帶有政治色彩的稱呼，類似「同志」或「同伴」，不是一般日常英文裡最常見的叫法。
generatedExample: The officer addressed the crowd as comrade.

explanationZhTw: 軍事口令，意思是「稍息」或「放輕鬆站著」；屬於固定用語，不是單純逐字理解。
generatedExample: After the drill, the captain told the soldiers to stand at ease.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「break out」不是「打破逃出」，而是指情況突然發作、突然出現；用來描述汗水一下子冒出來。
generatedExample: A rash broke out on my arm after the hike.

explanationZhTw: 「inscrutable」表示看不透的、難以理解的，通常用來形容表情、態度或動機讓人猜不出來。
generatedExample: Her expression stayed inscrutable during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「give you away」在這裡是指暴露、洩漏你的真實感受或秘密，不是單純「給出去」。
generatedExample: One nervous laugh could give him away.

explanationZhTw: 「comrades」是「同志、同伴」的意思。這裡是帶有集體/政治色彩的稱呼，用來叫同一陣營的人。
generatedExample: The manager addressed the team as comrades and asked everyone to work together.

explanationZhTw: 「capable of」表示「有能力做某事、能夠」。這裡強調年紀較輕的人做得到這個動作。
generatedExample: She is capable of finishing the report before noon.

explanationZhTw: 「front line」在這裡指「前線、最危險的作戰位置」。屬於戰爭語境中的用法，也帶有宣傳性的語氣。
generatedExample: The nurses on the front line worked long hours during the crisis.

explanationZhTw: 「keep fit」是固定說法，意思是「保持健康、維持體能」。
generatedExample: I jog after work to keep fit.

explanationZhTw: 「put up with」是片語動詞，意思是「忍受、容忍」不愉快的事。
generatedExample: I don't know how she puts up with the noise every night.
